name: Anti-GD3 immunotherapy attenuated fibrosis and bone aging signals
summary: GD3 adds a newer senescence immune-checkpoint target with mouse fibrosis and bone-remodeling effects.
statement: GD3 upregulation on senescent cells suppressed NK-cell-mediated immunosurveillance, and targeting GD3-positive senescent cells with anti-GD3 immunotherapy attenuated experimentally induced or age-related lung and liver fibrosis and age-related bone remodeling in mice.
population_or_model: Human and mouse senescent cell systems plus mouse fibrosis and bone-remodeling models
time_horizon: Preclinical fibrosis and age-related tissue-remodeling treatment windows
quantitative_note: The source reports GD3 upregulation with aging in liver, lung, kidney, and bone contexts and mouse attenuation of fibrosis and bone-remodeling phenotypes after anti-GD3 immunotherapy.
caveats: GD3 biology may not be senescent-cell-exclusive.
Fibrosis and bone remodeling are bounded tissue contexts, not broad aging efficacy.
